MEXICO | FINSA acquires $434M industrial portfolio, including three AAA-class buildings leased to Mercado Libre

(El Financiero)

$MELI's former CFO, Pedro Arnt back in 2021 when asked about $SE: 

“Look, on the competitive front, I think we always say the market has been competitive, it's a huge opportunity, it will continue to be competitive. And rather than looking at how many years of a head start do we have, it's how can we move consistently faster and accelerate our own pace of rollout of our network to try to continue to drive up same and next day delivery, continue to expand free shipping. So, I think the playbook so far, we've been very successful. And we just need to make sure that we don't get complacent. And we continue to do everything in our power to drive down cost, return a part of that improved cost back to consumers in the form of more free shipping, and continue to roll out the network efficiently so that we can get faster and faster. And I think it's an ongoing race, and we don't want to measure how far ahead we are, because that could breed complacency.”

And the playbook continues to this day. $MELI plans on investing over 80% of Shopee’s estimated 2025 Brazil GMV on its operations specifically in Brazil.

$MELI issued nearly 3 million new credit cards in a single quarter. Some investors see this rapid credit expansion and worry about risk. There is a bigger picture. This is not short-term noise. It is about locking in LTV

First, let's look at the raw numbers from Q4 2025. The total credit portfolio nearly doubled YoY to $12.5 billion. They successfully pushed out 3 million new credit cards right during the peak holiday shopping season.

Why issue so many cards? It is not just about collecting interest or late fees. The real goal is marketplace stickiness. The credit segment is the ultimate funnel to keep users inside the MELI ecosystem.

When a shopper gets a Mercado Pago card, their behavior changes entirely.
Management confirmed this in their latest earnings call. Once a user adopts the credit card, their overall engagement goes up. Their total net spend on the ecom platform rises significantly.

The margins are also much better. When a user pays with a 3P card, there is friction. When they pay with a Mercado Pago card on MercadoLibre, the company wins twice. Management actually calls this "double dipping."

MELI controls the checkout platform. MELI also controls the credit facility. Because they own both sides of the transaction, processing costs drop. At the same time, transaction approval rates skyrocket. This structurally raises the profit per customer.

The final piece of the puzzle is retention. Mercado Pago just hit a historic milestone. They now hold the number one Net Promoter Score in Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, and Chile.

Internal data shows a direct link: when users adopt credit products, their NPS and retention rates climb. They get access to extra installments and better cashback. They have no reason to shop anywhere else.

MercadoLibre's credit growth is not just an isolated, risky revenue stream. It is a calculated ecosystem lock-in. They are using credit to acquire high-LTV customers who will buy more, cost less to serve, and never leave. The incredible growth in credit cards is a strong leading indicator for their future imo.
For just under $15 usd a month you can get free fast shipping, Netflix, Disney+, HBO max, Apple TV+ in Brazil via $MELI+ Mega. What an absurd lock-in. That’s not even the promo price.

IRSA Y MERCADO LIBRE INVIERTEN MÁS DE US$50 MILLONES EN NUEVAS OFICINAS

IRSA y Mercado Libre anunciaron la construcción de un nuevo edificio de oficinas en el barrio de Saavedra, dentro del Polo DOT, con una inversión superior a los US $50 millones. La obra contempla una superficie de 15.000 metros cuadrados y Mercado Libre ocupará alrededor del 80%.

El proyecto se integrará como una ampliación del edificio Zetta y se prevé que esté finalizado en un plazo de 30 meses. El nuevo edificio se suma a otros desarrollos impulsados por IRSA y refuerza el posicionamiento del Polo DOT como un hub corporativo y tecnológico en expansión

Para Mercado Libre, el desarrollo forma parte del plan de inversión de US $3.400 millones que la compañía anunció para este año en Argentina.
